_about: Voiceover scripts for the 30 cellauto reels. Single-speaker, conversational, ~10-13s each. Fed to VibeVoice (microsoft/VibeVoice) to synthesize one WAV per reel; see README.md.
voice_direction: Warm, curious science-communicator. Confident, not breathless. Slight wonder.

reels.script: What you're watching is cell division, with no biology at all. Just four numbers in a reaction-diffusion equation, splitting like living protocells. That's where cellauto begins.
Two chemicals. One feeds, one decays, and they spread at different speeds. That tiny imbalance is enough to grow life-like patterns. Alan Turing predicted this back in 1952.
Change just two numbers, and the whole world changes. Spots become stripes, then waves, then mazes. One equation, five completely different regimes. Drag the sliders and watch it morph.
Before life, there was soup. cellauto seeds its primordial mix using the real measured yields from the 1953 Miller-Urey experiment. Chemistry first; life comes later.
Life may have started by plugging into a battery. At deep-sea vents, a proton gradient powers the first chemistry. cellauto reads out the real voltage and free energy, live.
What if molecules could make each other? That's an autocatalytic set; chemistry that bootstraps itself into existence. cellauto finds them with the proper Hordijk-Steel algorithm.
Why is life left-handed? A tiny chance imbalance, amplified until one mirror image wins completely. This is Frank's 1953 model of broken symmetry, running in real time.
In the RNA world, life balanced on a knife-edge. Too many copying errors, and the whole population melts down. Watch Eigen's error catastrophe unfold across the grid.
How did the genetic code write itself? Code, translation, and selection feed back on each other until the mapping locks in. cellauto shows that convergence, step by step.
Before membranes, there were droplets. Oily blobs that separate from water all on their own; coacervates. cellauto models them with Cahn-Hilliard phase separation.
Life on the rocks, literally. Clay surfaces concentrate molecules and catalyze the first polymers. cellauto pins reactions to montmorillonite, just like Ferris showed.
Every cell needs a wall. Above a critical concentration, fatty acids snap together into bubbles; vesicles. cellauto builds them with real bending physics and measured chemistry.
Natural selection, before DNA existed. Protocells with better internal chemistry outcompete the rest. cellauto runs the actual Eigen-Schuster hypercycle inside each one.
Meet your four-billion-year-old ancestor. By finding the genes shared across all life, cellauto distills LUCA, the last universal common ancestor, from the network itself.
Soup to LUCA, in twelve coupled stages. Each one hands its output to the next, so the whole origin of life plays as a single, continuous story.
The one that started it all: Conway's Game of Life. Four simple rules, infinite complexity. cellauto ships it as a reference point for everything else.
Four cells of memory. One simple rule. And yet, rule 110 is Turing-complete, capable of any computation. Simplicity hiding universal power.
Here's what makes cellauto different. Every single constant traces back to a published measurement. No hand-waving; real numbers, fully cited, in the docs.
Same seed, same universe, every single time. cellauto saves its random state, so a run reproduces bit-for-bit, even across save and load. Science you can repeat.
Want to try it right now? A live version runs in your browser. No install; just sliders and the reaction-diffusion equation painting patterns in real time.
How often does a project audit itself? cellauto's version 3.5 hunted down its own weak claims and fixed every one. Rare, refreshing honesty in software.
Twelve windows into genesis. Every panel is a real frame from the simulator, arranged as a museum plate. Where artificial life meets fine art.
This is the magnum opus. The entire twelve-stage origin of life, compressed into one poster. Every medallion is genuine simulator output.
Catalytic Silence: a design philosophy. Real science, presented like an observational plate from an old natural-history museum. Quietly beautiful.
This single frame holds the whole mystery. A protocell caught mid-fission, made by just four numbers. The image that defines the project.
Free, open, and MIT-licensed. Python, a hundred and forty-one passing tests, green CI. Fork it, run it, build on it, today.
Science for everyone. Colorblind-safe palettes, a reduced-motion mode, and full keyboard navigation. cellauto is built to be used by all.
Chemistry, to life, in twelve steps. From a warm pool of molecules, all the way to the last common ancestor. Abiogenesis you can actually run.
There's an edge to life. Push the mutation rate past the threshold, and information dissolves into noise. cellauto puts that boundary right on your screen.
Watch life build itself. Twelve stages, soup to LUCA, every frame real science. This is cellauto. Star it on GitHub, and run it yourself.
